intro.title: Linear Regression Concepts
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Linear Regression Concepts is a core topic when learning R for data analysis, statistics, and reproducible research.

Practicing this topic builds fluency with R syntax, data structures, and the tidyverse ecosystem.

Run the sample code below, experiment in the editor, then work through the challenges.
intro.sandboxCode: print("Model continuous outcome with predictors: y ~ x1 + x2")

challenges.title: Easy: Use print
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write R code that uses `print` related to **Linear Regression Concepts**.
challenges.starterCode: print("Model continuous outcome with predictors: y ~ x1 + x2")

tests.label: Correct output
tests.assertion: identical(trimws(_cq_stdout), "Model continuous outcome with predictors: y ~ x1 + x2")

challenges.title: Easy: Print a number
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print the number `100` using `print()`.

challenges.title: Easy: Assign a variable
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create variable `x` with `<-` and set it to `5`, then print `x`.

challenges.title: Medium: Apply Linear Regression Concepts
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write code demonstrating **Linear Regression Concepts**.
challenges.starterCode: print("Model continuous outcome with predictors: y ~ x1 + x2")

challenges.title: Medium: Two-step snippet
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `print` and `print` in one script.

challenges.title: Medium: Vector basics
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create `v <- c(1, 2, 3)` and print `sum(v)`.

challenges.title: Hard: Conditional output
challenges.promptMarkdown: Set `n <- 10`. If `n > 0`, print `"positive"`.

challenges.title: Hard: Function call
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `length()` on `c(4, 8, 15)` and print the result.

challenges.title: Expert: Topic practice
challenges.promptMarkdown: Combine ideas from **Linear Regression Concepts** in a short script using `print`.

challenges.title: Expert: Multi-line script
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write at least two lines of R related to **Linear Regression Concepts** (include `print`).
challenges.starterCode: print("Model continuous outcome with predictors: y ~ x1 + x2")

questions.prompt: Linear regression models:

choices.label: Only categorical labels with trees only

intro.title: Model Selection
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Model Selection is a core topic when learning R for data analysis, statistics, and reproducible research.

Practicing this topic builds fluency with R syntax, data structures, and the tidyverse ecosystem.

Run the sample code below, experiment in the editor, then work through the challenges.
intro.sandboxCode:
fit1 <- lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars)
fit2 <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars)
AIC(fit1, fit2)

challenges.title: Easy: Use AIC
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write R code that uses `AIC` related to **Model Selection**.
